summary: This plan proposes a set of modifiable BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers designed to reduce sudden anger spikes, lower bodily arousal during episodes, improve predictability of responses, align behavior with self-identity, and strengthen social support. By targeting arousal regulation, sleep/nutrition, emotion regulation skills, trigger rehearsal, impulse control, and identity-motivational factors, the approach aims to decrease episode frequency/severity and reduce post-episode shame, while improving readiness to change and long-term well-being.

solutions.predictor_variable: In-episode autonomic-arousal regulation toolkit (paced breathing and grounding)
solutions.solution_candidate: Real-time autonomic regulation using paced diaphragmatic breathing with a longer exhale, brief grounding (5 senses or 5-4-3-2-1), and optional biofeedback cue during episodes.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces peak bodily arousal, dampens trigger amplification, and lowers surprise by stabilizing physiological responses and grounding attention.
solutions.measurement: Episode-level peak arousal (0-10), episode count per week, and self-reported use of regulation technique (yes/no) per episode.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; instruct properly; monitor for dizziness or hyperventilation during initial practice.
solutions.mapping_query: Maps S01 to C02 (peak arousal during episodes) and C03 (trigger sensitivity) via in-the-moment regulation; aims to decrease response intensity and surprise.

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep regularization and caffeine/modulation for baseline arousal
solutions.solution_candidate: Stabilize sleep-wake schedule, limit late caffeine, maintain hydration, and establish regular meals to reduce baseline irritability and arousal.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep and moderated stimulant use lower baseline arousal and reactivity to minor triggers, reducing both intensity and frequency of episodes.
solutions.measurement: Sleep quantity/quality (logs or PSQI), daily caffeine intake, weekly episode count and peak intensity.
solutions.safety_notes: Be mindful of sleep disorders or caffeine sensitivity; consult clinician if sleep problems persist.
solutions.mapping_query: Addresses C02 baseline arousal and C03 trigger sensitivity; improved sleep reduces surprise and reactivity.

solutions.predictor_variable: Emotion regulation skills training (CBT/DBT) and cognitive reframing
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured weekly training in labeling emotions, adaptive appraisal, problem-solving, and cognitive reframe strategies; practice in daily life.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances top-down control of emotions, aligns behavior with values, and increases readiness to change by reducing unexpected anger and self-identity conflict.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of emotion regulation use (brief diary or scale), perceived identity-behavior alignment, readiness-to-change rating.
solutions.safety_notes: None beyond standard therapy supervision; monitor for distress during exposure exercises.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C04 (unexpectedness), C06 (identity conflict), and C08 (motivation) via structured emotion regulation training.

solutions.predictor_variable: Trigger-specific cognitive rehearsal and pre-commitment to response delay
solutions.solution_candidate: Mental rehearsal of calm, non-harmful responses to common triggers; set a pre-commitment to pause before reacting (e.g., 5-second rule) and use coping steps.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Pre-emptive cognitive preparation reduces trigger amplification, lowers episode frequency, and decreases surprise by establishing expected response patterns.
solutions.measurement: Number of trigger-provoked episodes per week, time to de-escalate, self-reported perceived surprise during episodes.
solutions.safety_notes: None; ensure practice under supervision if there is risk of escalation with certain triggers.
solutions.mapping_query: Connects C03 (trigger sensitivity), C01 (episode frequency), and C04 (unexpectedness) via pre-emptive cognitive rehearsal.

solutions.predictor_variable: Urge-surfing and delay of responding during anger
solutions.solution_candidate: Training in pausing for 5-10 seconds during anger, labeling emotion, breathing, and choosing non-harmful actions; escalate with safety plan if needed.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves inhibitory control to prevent outward aggression and reduces recurrence by interrupting automatic responding.
solutions.measurement: Incidents of outward aggression, average delay before responding, adherence to pause technique.
solutions.safety_notes: Develop a safety plan; involve therapist if risk of harm increases.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C07 (inhibition) and C01 (episode frequency) through urge-surfing and response delay training.

solutions.predictor_variable: Identity-aligned values exploration and self-concept work
solutions.solution_candidate: Values clarification and identity consolidation exercises to reduce perceived mismatch between anger responses and the self-image.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthening value-aligned behavior reduces cognitive dissonance after episodes and boosts motivation to change.
solutions.measurement: Identity congruence score, readiness-to-change rating, self-reported values alignment.
solutions.mapping_query: Addresses C06 (identity mismatch) and C08 (motivation) via values-focused identity work.

solutions.solution_candidate: Shame- and self-compassion–focused practices to reduce post-episode shame and self-criticism; normalize emotions.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces self-criticism and identity threat after episodes, supporting ongoing motivation to change and identity repair.
solutions.measurement: Post-episode shame rating, self-compassion scale, motivation score.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C05 (shame), C06 (identity), and C08 (motivation) through shame resilience and self-compassion work.

solutions.predictor_variable: Social support and relational repair interventions
solutions.solution_candidate: Engage supportive social networks; involve trusted others in monitoring and accountability; family or partner sessions as appropriate.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social connectedness buffers emotional reactions, reduces isolation, and reinforces identity-consistent behavior and motivation.
solutions.measurement: Perceived social support scale, frequency of supportive interactions, post-episode shame and identity alignment reports.
solutions.safety_notes: Set healthy boundaries; check for risk of relational conflict or abuse; involve clinician if needed.
solutions.mapping_query: Supports C08 (motivation), C05 (shame), and C06 (identity) via enhanced social connectedness.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured weekly aerobic activity (e.g., brisk walking, cycling) to improve autonomic regulation and mood.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Exercise improves autonomic balance and mood, reducing arousal during episodes and lowering frequency.
solutions.measurement: Weekly active minutes, pre/post episode arousal, weekly episode count.
solutions.safety_notes: Progress gradually; consult clinician for medical conditions; monitor for signs of overtraining.
solutions.mapping_query: Affects C02 (arousal), C03 (trigger sensitivity), and C01 (episode frequency) via improved physio-regulation and mood.

solutions.predictor_variable: Nutrition, micronutrient balance and gut-brain axis support
solutions.solution_candidate: Dietary patterns rich in anti-inflammatory foods, omega-3 Fatty acids, magnesium; hydration; GI health considerations.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Nutritional state supports brain regulation, reduces irritability, and enhances cognitive control and motivation.
solutions.measurement: Diet quality score, adherence to supplement plan, mood/anger ratings.
solutions.safety_notes: Check allergies; consult dietitian for medical conditions; avoid unsafe supplements.
solutions.mapping_query: Biological mood regulation via nutrition impacting arousal (C02), identity alignment (C06), and motivation (C08).
